mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, confirming robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising input costs, which erode profit margins and trigger immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods do not precede negative 5-day forward returns, with a win rate below 40% over a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real interest rates are rising sufficiently to cool price pressures without triggering a hard landing, creating a favorable risk environment that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL spread shows no predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the regression coefficient over any 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer purchasing power, which flows into corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth rates exhibit a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple economic cycles, failing to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% confirms an official recession, causing immediate repricing of equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as investors liquidate positions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts by over 0.5%, invalidating the recession repricing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable employment during a post-shock recovery phase signals efficient capacity utilization and margin expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising INDPRO and stable PAYEMS fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in a multivariate regression framework.
